verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient was originally diagnosed with non-small cell lung cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This can be found in the OpenEMR patient chart (Onc Patient 7), tab Clinical Notes, row 33, column E, which says, "stage I non-small cell lung cancer." 

This is also supported by the script. "I’m sorry the lung cancer you developed several years ago has returned."

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has now been diagnosed with small-cell lung cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This requires synthesizing information from the attached script. "When compared to the original diagnosis, it has undergone a transformation. We believe this was a response to the EGFR therapy you received during your initial treatment several years ago." 

NSCLC can transform into SCLC in some cases as a resistance mechanism. "The histological transformation of NSCLC into SCLC has been reported as an important mechanism of EGFR-TKI-resistance." https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10646212/

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's cancer is metastatic.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This can be found in the attached patient script. "You were diagnosed in the past with lung cancer in the right upper lobe. Now, that same cancer has spread to some lymph nodes and a small spot in the opposite lung. We believe there might be a few small spots in your abdomen as well." This indicates metastasis.

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
Based on the degree of metastasis indicated in the script, this patient's disease is considered extensive stage. 

"Extensive stage. This describes cancers that have spread widely throughout the lung, to the other lung, or to other parts of the body." https://www.cancer.org/cancer/types/lung-cancer/detection-diagnosis-staging/staging-sclc.html

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has received systemic therapy.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This can be found in the attached script. "We gave you platinum-based chemotherapy with etoposide and a PD-L1 inhibitor." 

Chemotherapy qualifies as systemic therapy. "Chemotherapy is usually systemic." 
https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/treatments/16859-chemotherapy

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's cancer lacks any targetable mutations.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This can be found in the attached lab results, which show all negative results on genetic testing.

"ALK Rearrangement Negative Negative
ROS1 Rearrangement Negative Negative
RET fusion Negative Negative
EGFR Exon 20 Insertion Negative Negative
NTRK fusion Negative Negative
MET skipping mutation Negative Negative
MET amplification Negative Negative
BRAF V600E Negative Negative
HER2 Negative Negative
KRAS G12C Negative Negative
CD74NRG1 Negative Negative
EGFR Exon 19 del Negative Negative"

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has preserved organ function.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This is stated in the attached script and supported by the patient's lab results showing a normal set of labs. This indicates organ function is still normal.

"Fortunately, your organs are still functioning well, and we do not see any signs of interstitial lung disease just yet."

verifier_values.criteria: States that the most appropriate next treatment option is to start tarlatamab.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The patient has extensive stage small cell lung cancer with disease progression after platinum-based chemotherapy, and with preserved organ function and no targetable mutations. The patient meets all indications to start tarlatamab. 

"Tarlatamab is a bispecific delta-like ligand 3 (DLL3)-directed CD3 T-cell engager indicated for the treatment of adult patients with extensive stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) with disease progression on or after platinum-based chemotherapy." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2024/761344s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that tarlatamab is dosed intravenously.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the route of administration of tarlatamab. "for intravenous use." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2024/761344s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient should receive 1 mg of tarlatamab on day 1.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the dosing of tarlatamab. "Day 1, Step-Up Dose, 1mg." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2024/761344s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient should receive 10 mg of tarlatamab on day 8.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the dosing of tarlatamab. "Day 8, Step-Up Dose, 10mg." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2024/761344s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient should receive 10 mg of tarlatamab on day 15.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the dosing of tarlatamab. "Day 15, Step-Up Dose, 10mg." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2024/761344s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient should receive 10 mg of tarlatamab biweekly after the third dose.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This is the dosing of tarlatamab. "Cycle 2: Day 1 and 15, 10 mg." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2024/761344s000lbl.pdf 

This cycle continues for cycles 3 and 4.
